summary_text:
Chief Complaint
The patient reports concerns regarding her daughter's kidney health and a desire to ensure her kidneys are evaluated.

History of Present Illness
The patient's daughter, Katherine, was born prematurely at 34 and 3-week gestational age as twins. She experienced feeding difficulties at birth, requiring intervention. She has been diagnosed with Smith-Magenis syndrome, which is associated with congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract. The patient's son also has autism. She has been referred by her geneticist for a kidney ultrasound to evaluate for potential congenital anomalies. She has been treated for reflex and has been taking cow's milk protein free formula. She has seen an allergist, neurologist, and has been receiving physical, occupational, speech, and feeding therapy. She has been seeing an orthopedic and ophthalmologist. She has also been evaluated by an audiologist. The patient reports that she has experienced a whirlwind of medical appointments and therapies. She has been drinking approximately 8 to 10 diapers per day and reports drinking a significant amount of fluids.

Findings
The patient appears well-nourished and is slightly fussy during examination. Her eyes appear small. She demonstrates mild hypotonia of the lower extremities. External genitalia appear normal. The patient's heart rate and respiratory rate are within normal limits. Physical exam reveals no acute findings.

Assessment
Katherine is a 22-month-old female with Smith-Magenis syndrome, presenting for evaluation of potential congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract. The patient's history is consistent with this syndrome's associated complications.

Plan
We will obtain a kidney ultrasound today to evaluate for congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract. If abnormalities are identified, we will determine the next steps and schedule appropriate follow-up. The family lives in Dallas, Georgia, so her follow-up should be at the Town Center location. The nurse will provide instructions for the ultrasound and complete necessary paperwork.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Renal screening tests.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Katherine Lopez is a 22-month-old female, born at 34 weeks +3 days gestation, diagnosed with the rare Smith-Magenis syndrome and associated global developmental delays. She was referred to nephrology by genetics who recommended renal screening tests. At birth, the patient weighed 4 pounds 8 ounces, exhibited difficulties during eating where she would stop breathing, and challenges regulating her body temperature; determined to be typical issues associated with premature birth. The patient has seen many specialists, including GI for severe reflux, a dietitian who recommended discontinuing consumption of cow’s milk, and a neurologist who diagnosed her with Smith-Magenis syndrome. Additionally, she has been seen by a geneticist, orthopedist, ophthalmologist, and audiologist. The patient is not ambulatory, did not sit up unassisted until 1 years old, and is currently crawling and attempting to take steps. She will be starting speech therapy in the next few weeks, and currently she is in occupational, feeding, and physical therapy. The geneticist informed the patient's mother that patients with Smith-Magenis syndrome usually have kidney issues where “the kidneys fuse into one” and issues with their legs.

The patient has approximately 8 to 10 wet diapers in a 24-hour period. Her mother notes that she is doing better with drinking fluids and she urinates “a lot”. The patient drinks Ripple Pea Protein milk instead of cow’s milk, water and water mixed with a small amount of juice. Her twin sister is approximately the same size, and their weight is essentially the same, she is healthy, and the mom denies any known health concerns or diagnoses. The mom states that Katherine is shorter and seems chubbier related to her syndrome. She also has a 9-year-old brother who is diagnosed with autism.

BIRTH HISTORY

Twin gestation. Birth weight 4 pounds 8 ounces.

PAST HISTORY

Medical
Smith-Magenis syndrome.

SOCIAL HISTORY

Patient accompanied to appointment by her mother.
Sibling: 9-year-old brother and twin sister.

FAMILY HISTORY

Brother: 9 years old, positive for autism.
Twin sister: absence of Smith-Magenis syndrome, no known health conditions.
No known family history of genetic conditions, kidney failure, dialysis, or kidney transplant.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Constitutional
Well-nourished female, slightly fussy when examined.

Eyes
Presences of microphthalmia.

Genitourinary
Normal external female genitalia.

Musculoskeletal
Extremities: Presence of mild hypotonia of lower extremities and the arms.

ASSESSMENT

• Smith-Magenis syndrome.
Katherine is a 22-month-old former 34+3-week-old twin with Smith-Magenis syndrome.
Several organ systems can be affected by this chromosomal deletion syndrome and congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract have been reported in the literature.

PLAN

We will obtain a kidney ultrasound screening today. If there are abnormalities on the kidney ultrasound, we will determine the next steps and future follow-up. The family lives in Dallas, Georgia, so her follow-up should be at the Town Center location.

INSTRUCTIONS

Complete ultrasound today. I will call patient with results and possible next steps.

section_miscategorizations.text: She has been treated for reflex and has been taking cow's milk protein free formula.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This describes prior/ongoing treatment rather than current symptoms or chronology; it would more cleanly fit under treatment history/management, but its presence in HPI does not substantially mislead.

section_miscategorizations.text: She has seen an allergist, neurologist, and has been receiving physical, occupational, speech, and feeding therapy. She has been seeing an orthopedic and ophthalmologist. She has also been evaluated by an audiologist.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are prior and ongoing specialist care and therapies (management history). While often summarized in HPI, they conceptually align more with treatment/management history than with symptom chronology.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that she has experienced a whirlwind of medical appointments and therapies.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is more of a contextual/psychosocial observation about care burden than a symptom description; it could reasonably be placed in an exam/observations or social/context section, but its placement in HPI is not misleading.
